response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior full-stack engineer with strong breadth (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, web/mobile/embedded) and some leadership and mentoring background, but the role explicitly requires 8 years of C++ experience and 5 years of large-scale infrastructure/distributed systems work in that ecosystem. The resume shows only a ~6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and primary distributed-systems work in DLang rather than C++. Core technical requirements are not met.
strengths: Broad polyglot experience across backend, web, mobile, and embedded systems
Track record of mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership (GISCollective, Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
Exposure to high-throughput micro-services and software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 6 months — far below the 8-year minimum
Distributed-systems work was primarily in DLang/Node.js, not C++ cloud compute platforms
No demonstrated production cloud-compute platform ownership or formal tech-lead role at the required scale

key_factors.factor: Large-scale distributed systems exposure (wrong language stack)
Mentoring, OSS leadership, and software-craftsmanship background
